name: Cumberland County Militia Regiment
notes: Appointed by NC Provincial Congress, September 9, 1775. Active through end of war.

name: Highland Loyalist Column
notes: Assembled Cross Creek, January–February 1776. Approximately 1,500 at muster, reduced to ~700–800 by time of battle. Commanded nominally by BG Donald MacDonald (ill); effective command passed to LTC Donald MacLeod before the battle. Disbanded and largely captured after Moore's Creek Bridge.

name: Caswell's NC Minutemen
notes: Colonel Richard Caswell commanding. Approximately 800 men. Occupied west bank camp, withdrew across bridge before dawn February 27, participated in defense of east bank earthworks and subsequent pursuit.

name: Lillington's Wilmington Minutemen
notes: Colonel Alexander Lillington commanding. Approximately 150 men. Constructed and held the east bank earthworks. NPS archaeology confirms earthworks exactly on original position: roughly 4ft high, 7ft wide, 2ft ditch outside.

name: Ashe's New Hanover Rangers
notes: Captain John Ashe commanding. Approximately 100 men. New Hanover Volunteer Company of Rangers. Caswell's report grouped Ashe's men with Lillington's force, causing undercounts in some early sources.

name: Patriot Combined Artillery
notes: Mother Covington (3-pounder) and daughter (half-pounder) positioned behind earthworks. Additional 6 or 9-pounders per NPS park historians. Several pieces loaded with swanshot — tin canisters spreading approximately 20 small bullets on impact.

name: Loyalist Right Wing
notes: Approximately 400 men. Advanced toward bridge; found Caswell's camp empty with fires still burning; believed Patriots in retreat. A flag-of-truce messenger had earlier scouted Patriot position at midnight.

name: Loyalist Left Wing
notes: Approximately 300 men. Saw empty Patriot camp with fires burning; fell back into woods to consult MacDonald; did NOT participate in the charge. As AmRevNC notes: literally the right hand did not know what the left hand was doing.

name: Campbell's Highland Advance Company
notes: Captain John Campbell commanding. Approximately 75 picked broadswordsmen. Led charge across dismantled, greased bridge stringers. Yelled 'King George and Broadswords.' Campbell killed at or near east bank.

name: MacLeod's Command
notes: LTC Donald MacLeod commanding. Led charge with Campbell. KIA on bridge. Sources disagree on wound count: Moore AAR says 'upwards of twenty balls'; NCpedia says 9 bullets and 24 swan shot; Wikipedia says 20 musket balls.

name: Patriot Combined Force — Earthworks
notes: Combined Caswell and Lillington forces holding east bank earthworks during the action. Approximately 950 total. Held fire until Loyalists within approximately 30 paces; opened with muskets and artillery simultaneously. Battle lasted approximately 3 minutes.

name: Patriot Pursuit — Main
notes: Patriots replaced bridge planks and crossed in pursuit of retreating Loyalists. Colonel Moore arrived on scene a few hours after the battle.

name: Patriot Pursuit — Flanking Company
notes: One company led by one of Caswell's lieutenants. Forded creek above bridge to flank retreating Loyalists. Officer unnamed in all sources consulted.
